items.title: Berkeley Vale mother Louise Farley took up soccer for her dying son
items.summary: After Spencer Farley was diagnosed with terminal cancer, he asked his mum to start playing soccer. In the 12 months since his death, the game has kept her going.

items.title: Train station car parking in Melbourne's west becomes election issue
items.summary: With only months to the Victorian election, Melton Council is calling on both sides of politics to commit to two new train stations in the fast-growing municipality.

items.title: FIFA president Gianni Infantino accused of 'destroying the football industry' with World Cup expansion
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino comes in for more heavy criticism, this time at the hands of La Liga boss Javier Tebas.

items.title: $30m biochar facility proposed in central Queensland, with Rio Tinto a major buyer
items.summary: Rio Tinto has signed an agreement with an Australian company for a biochar product that could help replace coal used in its emissions-intensive Queensland refineries.

items.title: Boy, 16, charged with murdering mother, attempting to kill sister at Bentley home
items.summary: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

items.title: CLP government passes amended child protection laws in 'significant overhaul' for Northern Territory
items.summary: The Northern Territory parliament has passed changes to child protection laws, with the Country Liberal Party describing its amendments as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

items.title: Bradley died months after national antipsychotic medicine shortages, coronial inquest hears
items.summary: Bradley Buswell, aged in his 40s and living with schizophrenia, died in state care after his medication became unavailable due to national shortages. An ongoing coronial inquest is probing the circumstances of his death.

items.title: Elusive fogbow photographed at Cobar, western NSW
items.summary: Cold weather and moist conditions combine to produce a fogbow in an unlikely place.

items.title: Players and fans made this World Cup a success, but the politics shouldn't be forgotten
items.summary: The biggest ever World Cup brought diverse communities together and galvanised its non-traditional footballing co-hosts, but the concerns raised from the start have not disappeared in the positive afterglow.

items.title: Hegseth reveals $US37.5b cost of Iran war during grilling over testosterone policy, troop deaths
items.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is being questioned on the cost of the war against Iran, his idea of testosterone screening for troops, and the number of service people who have died.

items.title: Rebel Wilson awaits ruling in defamation battle with fellow actor
items.summary: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson will learn today whether she defamed the lead actor on her "fun girl-power" film, in a judgment to be handed down in the Federal Court.

items.title: Outback reunions cancelled by rain damage as tourism suffers for second year
items.summary: Flood-damaged roads and soggy racetracks mean some of the outback's favourite events are being cancelled and keeping tourists away.

items.title: Whyalla workers injured amid attempts to restart steelworks' blast furnace
items.summary: Three Whyalla steelworks employees have been treated for injuries suffered during attempts to restart the site's troubled blast furnace, the ABC understands.

items.title: BlueScope Steel hit with multiple workplace safety breaches after contractor's death
items.summary: There have been six incidents involving falling tools and building materials at BlueScope, less than a year since contractor Jack McGrath was killed.

items.title: JackJumpers Roth's advice for inaugural Tasmania Devils AFL coach Ken Hinkley
items.summary: Being out amongst the people will be key for incoming Tasmania Devils coach Ken Hinkley, according to the man who led the state to NBL glory in 2024.

items.title: Woman's family wrongly told she was dead in hospital identity error
items.summary: A Melbourne hospital apologises after mixing up the medical data of two people, resulting in one of them being wrongly declared dead by police.

items.title: Artificial intelligence company Firmus plans to use state-owned agricultural irrigation water
items.summary: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

items.title: Albanese survived three near-fatal car accidents. His career is just as unlikely
items.summary: Anthony Albanese has been in near-fatal vehicle accidents not once, but three times. Politically, his is also a story of survival against the odds.

items.title: Jewish schools say kids hurting as security getting in way of education
items.summary: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears from Jewish school leaders about how they need to implement extraordinary and expensive security measures.

items.title: Family of seven's home uninhabitable after Suncorp insurance delay exacerbates damage
items.summary: A Queensland family is living in an overcrowded caravan and shed after insurance provider Suncorp exacerbated damage to their home, initially caused by a leak.

items.title: Penny Lane is Dead pairs gory horror with a queer love story
items.summary: After spending years in special effects and make-up, filmmaker Mia'Kate Russell's ocker nightmare Penny Lane Is Dead has made it to the silver screen.

items.title: Travel quiz, July 22: Join us for a trip around the world
items.summary: Where are macadamia nuts indigenous to? And what’s the capital city of Switzerland?

items.title: Trump says US will target suspected Iranian nuclear site 'pretty soon'
items.summary: Donald Trump says the United States is far from finished with Iran, warning its next military target could be an underground nuclear complex.

items.title: Killed UK politician Ann Widdecombe hit 21 times with hammer, prosecutors say
items.summary: Joshua Kerry, 28, appears in the Westminster Magistrates' Court charged with murder over a killing that has shocked the public and political establishment.

items.title: China's EV Revolution
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Inquest to determine whether Adelaide man killed his mother and staged her death
items.summary: An inquest is investigating whether a man killed his mother before placing her in the driver's seat of her car to stage that she was behind the wheel during a crash.

items.title: NSW Police officer locked teen in cell prior to alleged rape at station, court told
items.summary: A NSW Police officer locked a 17-year-old in a cell and handcuffed her on separate occasions before he sexually assaulted her in a police station in 1998, a court has heard.

items.title: Five teenagers plead guilty to manslaughter of Jack Westren at Shellharbour Junction
items.summary: Four boys and one girl have pleaded guilty to manslaughter, after a man with an acquired brain injury was lured to a train station on the NSW South Coast.

items.title: Hanson's European travel benefactor still a mystery, but One Nation leader says it was not taxpayers
items.summary: In her first appearance since returning to Australia, Pauline Hanson fields questions about her trip to Europe with billionaire Gina Rinehart.

items.title: Alessandra Galloni examines the implications of AI for journalism in 2026 Andrew Olle lecture
items.summary: What will a trusted, sustainable, global news organisation look like in an era of agentic AI? And can we reinvent ourselves before someone else builds a less trustworthy, more dangerous alternative?

items.title: Indonesia sentences leader of baby-trafficking ring to seven years' jail
items.summary: Lie Siu Luan, 70, was the leader of a group of 19 people accused of trafficking babies in Indonesia and Singapore.

items.title: Daily meals for Hobart's Risdon Prison shipped from Victoria as flooring fault shuts $19m kitchen
items.summary: A newly built commercial kitchen at Hobart's Risdon Prison has shut just three months after officially opening due to construction faults, leaving staff to source daily meals for about 840 inmates from Victoria.

items.title: Collingwood coach Craig McRae says Bobby Hill will 'always be welcome' at club as he gets set to cut contract
items.summary: With Collingwood set to be at full strength for its Adelaide clash, coach Craig McRae sends a message to missing star Bobby Hill, who is set to quit the club.

items.title: Hellenic Club found to have breached withdrawal regulations, cleared of failing to record problem gambling
items.summary: The Hellenic Club has been cleared of breaches of failing to record the problem gambling of a Canberra couple despite the pair losing at least $345,000 on poker machines.

items.title: AFL legend Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash
items.summary: AFL legend Tony Modra shares a post-crash update on social media, posting photos of him in hospital and with family, and saying he is now "getting out and about".

items.title: Federal Labor MP Luke Gosling 'categorically denies' incident with NT administrator
items.summary: Former army commando turned Labor MP, Luke Gosling, has denied involvement in an alleged incident with King Charles's representative in the NT.

items.title: Former Bishop of Broome Christopher Saunders faces first day of sex abuse trial
items.summary: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

items.title: Why supporters of India's 'Cockroach' movement marched on parliament
items.summary: Thousands of protesters under the banner of the Cockroach Janta Party have marched on India's parliament and clashed with the authorities. Here's why.

items.title: Kids do feel the cold. So why won't they wear a jumper?
items.summary: Do kids really not feel the cold like adults do? Or are they just expressing their independence? And when should you insist?

items.title: Queensland government spends $50 million on advertising in 2025–26 financial year
items.summary: Parliamentary budget estimates revealed the LNP administration spent more than $5 million on a statewide campaign on what it is delivering for Queensland.

items.title: Brisbane mother says extremely rare identical quadruplets 'one giant miracle'
items.summary: A maternal fetal medicine expert believes identical quadruplets born in Brisbane may be even rarer than one in 15 million, given they shared a placenta.

items.title: Tasmania's association of retired police question minister over delay of trauma support trial
items.summary: Tasmania's association of retired police officers has questioned the police minister over his commitment to the wellbeing of members after the stalling of a much-awaited trial that would offer trauma support.

items.title: Author Randa Abdel-Fattah appears to abandon threatened defamation case against SA premier
items.summary: Earlier this year, Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah said she had sent two concerns notices to SA Premier Peter Malinauskas over comments made around the time of her controversial removal from the line-up of this year's Adelaide Writers' Week.

items.title: Scam centres growing 'exponentially' despite Myanmar crackdown
items.summary: The scam industry in Myanmar is morphing and heading into the jungle rather than disappearing since a crackdown began.

items.title: Tradies and real estate agents among focus as police investigate alleged criminal syndicate
items.summary: Victoria Police say they will be targeting anyone who organised or assisted with an alleged drug and money laundering operation for a state-wide criminal syndicate.

items.title: Central Queensland University underpaid staff by $1.3 million, Fair Work finds
items.summary: Central Queensland University signs an enforceable undertaking to repay more than a thousand staff members who were affected by underpayments that date back to the 2012 enterprise agreement.

items.title: Surgical registration of Dr Liu-Ming Schmidt suspended after patient complaints
items.summary: A six-month ban on Dr Liu-Ming Schmidt, who works across multiple hospitals in southern NSW, comes after 13 patient complaints including a missed cancer diagnosis and a "catastrophic error" that contributed to a patient's death.

items.title: Wyndham mayor Preet Singh resigns over sex offender character reference
items.summary: Wyndham mayor Preet Singh says he showed "poor judgement" but also claims to have faced "abuse and intimidation" over the issue.

items.title: Police investigate stabbing of 15-year-old at Cranbourne North
items.summary: A boy was found with serious injuries and taken to hospital after an altercation between a group of teenagers.

items.title: Why more Australian men are choosing to be childfree
items.summary: About 15 per cent of Australian men say they don't want children, an increase from 11 per cent in 2005. Ian says his own childhood played a large part in his decision.

items.title: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson denies advocacy for 'version' of White Australia policy
items.summary: Pauline Hanson angrily denies suggestions her strident opposition to migration by anyone from "radical Islamic countries" amounts to a version of the White Australia policy.

items.title: Jake Weatherald retained as Australia reveals Test squad for Bangladesh series
items.summary: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

items.title: Australia's jobseeker system fails workers and enriches millionaires, report says
items.summary: A new report argues Australia's employment services system is failing jobseekers, while filling the pockets of private equity firms and millionaires.

items.title: Sixteen-year-old in custody suspected of killing mother, injuring little sister
items.summary: Police say a teenage boy is suspected to have stabbed his mother to death and seriously injured his 12-year-old sister in an alleged family violence homicide in Perth's south-east.

items.title: Nathan Buckley unlikely to join Essendon and Carlton coaching races after Tasmania snub
items.summary: Nathan Buckley still harbours ambitions of coaching a second AFL team despite being overlooked for the Tasmania job.

items.title: Spain's World Cup heroes greeted by millions at homecoming as FIFA probes ugly post-game fight
items.summary: Spain's triumphant World Cup team's homecoming lights up Madrid as FIFA investigates a series of altercations that took place after the final whistle.

items.title: Tiny red-fin blue-eye fish found only in Australia survives predator onslaught
items.summary: With scoops made from milk bottles, volunteers have been working tirelessly to save the red-fin blue-eye, found nowhere else in the world, from extinction.

items.title: Papua New Guinea moves to outlaw AI deepfakes as victims call for more protection
items.summary: Papua New Guinea prepares new laws to criminalise the malicious use of artificial intelligence, including deepfakes, voice cloning and digital impersonation.

items.title: Reg Tayler named Australia's new women's rugby sevens coach ahead of 2028 LA Olympics
items.summary: Rugby Australia makes a key appointment, as the world champion women's rugby sevens team looks ahead to the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

items.title: Whistleblower's fight to expose decades of child abuse by Katanning Hostel rapist Dennis McKenna
items.summary: Maggie Dawkins's efforts to shine a light on the horrific abuses committed by Dennis McKenna against a generation of boys in Katanning are set down in a new book.

items.title: Kevin Keegan dies aged 75, football world 'devastated' by loss of English icon
items.summary: Former Liverpool and Newcastle United star Kevin Keegan is remembered as one of English football's first rock stars after his death at 75.

items.title: Prime minister backs bid for future Northern Territory AFL team, but no promises on funding
items.summary: Anthony Albanese says he would love to see the creation of a Northern Territory AFL team while visiting the Tiwi Islands in the NT.

items.title: Hundreds attend police meeting about Donald teen Layla Jeffery's alleged murder
items.summary: Victoria Police and the Buloke Shire Council have held an event updating 300 Donald community members on the alleged murder of Layla Jeffery.

items.title: Farmer Peter Chauncy's firewood side hustle fuels petrol station success
items.summary: When bushfire and drought robbed farmer Peter Chauncy of his livelihood, he started chopping wood to sell. Forty years later, he runs one of Australia's largest firewood businesses.
